title: The Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) Curve
description: A graph plotting the True Positive Rate (Sensitivity) against the False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity) for a classification model at various classification thresholds. It illustrates the trade-off between the true positive rate and the false positive rate.
relationships: The ROC Curve Line shows the performance of the classifier; a curve closer to the top-left corner indicates better performance.
The Line of No Discrimination represents a random classifier; the AUC for this line is 0.5.
Each point on the ROC Curve corresponds to a specific classification threshold, balancing TPR and FPR.
The Area Under the Curve (AUC) provides a single metric to compare classifiers; a higher AUC (closer to 1.0) indicates a better model.
